header.description: Single-ship ammunition-depletion scenario pinning G4 finite ammunition (Step 7 guns, seal v1.13r0). AC0 (A6M2) flies wings-level due east along the equator (bank 0, n=1.0, throttle 0.8) and holds the trigger from tick 0 to the end. Its 20 mm cannon carries the roster's smallest magazine (ammo_start=100) and the slowest fire-rate (rof_interval_ticks=9), so with the decrement-then-fire cooldown it looses one round every 9 ticks: shots at ticks 0, 9, 18, ..., 891 (100 rounds), and at tick 891 the magazine reaches 0. From tick 892 on the gun is 'Winchester' (empty): the trigger is still held and the aircraft is alive, but firing is gated on ammo>0 so NO further rounds spawn and the projectile count stops growing. At tick 950 the final snapshot shows AC0 with ammo=0 (the 10th per-aircraft f64) and exactly the rounds fired in the last ttl=250 ticks still airborne (spawned at ticks 702..891). This is the FIRST golden to exercise the depletion gate: it proves an emptied magazine deterministically silences the gun, bit-for-bit cross-toolchain. Level equatorial flight keeps the aircraft far from the ground and inside +/-90 deg flight-path angle (no cos(gamma) singularity); firing at empty air means hp stays 70 (no self-hit, no target). NEW golden for G4.
header.introduced: first golden with an ammunition-depletion (Winchester) event; the 9 prior goldens move at v1.13r0 only because ammo (the 10th per-aircraft f64) is appended to every aircraft in the snapshot.
